subject: [PATCH 04/11] mm/zsmalloc: Introduce objcgs pointer in struct zspage

reviews.author: Nhat Pham
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested using vmalloc instead of kcalloc for allocating the objcgs array, allowing a fallback to vmalloc if necessary
reviews.raw_body:
I remembered asking this, so my apologies if I missed/forgot your
response - but would vmalloc work here? i.e kvcalloc to fallback to
vmalloc etc.?

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the vmalloc call should be changed to use GFP_KERNEL instead of GFP_HIGHMEM, agreed to make this change in v3.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Nhat : -)

Thank you for reviewing, and for your acks on the other parts!

You're right, I missed changing that on my end after v1. No reason
vmalloc shouldn't work here, let me make that change in v3.

Thanks, I hope you have a great day!
Joshua

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch introduces an array of obj_cgroup pointers in the zspage structure to track compressed objects' memory cgroup ownership, if the zs_pool is made memcg-aware at creation time. The error path for alloc_zspage has been simplified by moving it to a jump label. Additionally, the patch includes changes to handle zpdesc allocation and free_zpdesc calls in the error path.
